title: NAP Scholarship 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for Archivists of African Descent to Advance Their Careers and Projects
summary: The preservation of history, culture, and collective memory is a powerful tool for social justice, identity, and community empowerment. Recognizing the importance of inclusive archival practices, the Nomadic Archivists Project (NAP) has opened applications for the NAP Scholarship 2026 , a unique opportunity designed to support emerging and independent archivists of African descent. This annual scholarship aims to remove financial barriers and create access to professional development opportunities, enabling archivists to expand their skills, networks, and impact. Applications for the 2026 cycle are currently open and must be submitted by March 31, 2026 . About the NAP Scholarship The NAP Scholarship is a financial award of up to $1,500 , granted annually to support: Students in archival studies Early-career archivists Independent archivists The scholarship is specifically intended to help recipients either: Attend the Society of American Archivists (SAA) Annual Conference , or Fund an archival project that contributes to community memory and knowledge preservation By investing in archivists, the program contributes to a more inclusive and representative archival field. Why This Scholarship Matters Access to professional opportunities in archival studies is often limited by financial constraints. Conferences, training programs, and project development require resources that many emerging archivists may not have. The NAP Scholarship directly addresses these challenges by: Increasing access to professional networks Supporting skill development and education Enabling community-centered archival projects Promoting representation within archival institutions The initiative emphasizes that access to information and opportunities is essential for equity in the archival field . What the Scholarship Covers Selected recipients can use the funding for a wide range of expenses, including: Conference registration fees Travel and accommodation Memberships in professional organizations Training programs and workshops Archival supplies and project materials Personal time dedicated to project development This flexibility allows applicants to tailor the funding to their specific needs and career goals. Program Impact and Vision The NAP Scholarship is rooted in the belief that archivists play a critical role in shaping historical narratives and preserving marginalized voices. Through this initiative, the Nomadic Archivists Project seeks to: Empower archivists of African descent globally Foster community-driven archival practices Encourage ethical and inclusive documentation Strengthen participation in professional archival spaces The scholarship also highlights the importance of platforms like the Society of American Archivists (SAA), where archivists can: Share research and projects Build professional networks Participate in leadership opportunities Engage in workshops and training sessions Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the following requirements: Be a current undergraduate or graduate student , or an independent archivist Be of African descent Demonstrate a commitment to pursuing a career in archives Show a clear financial need for the scholarship Application Requirements To be considered for the NAP Scholarship 2026, applicants must submit: A 500–750 word essay outlining: Their interest in archival work, or A proposed archival project A resume A professional headshot Additional Requirement (if applicable): Applicants seeking funding for a project must include a detailed budget (e.g., supplies, travel, production costs) Application Process All applications must be submitted via email. 📧 Submission Email: miranda@nomadicarchivistsproject.com Applicants are encouraged to ensure that all required documents are complete and clearly presented before submission. For questions regarding eligibility or the application process, applicants can contact Miranda Mims via the same email address. Application Deadline Deadline to Apply: March 31, 2026 Late submissions may not be considered, so applicants are strongly advised to apply well in advance. Why You Should Apply The NAP Scholarship offers more than financial support—it provides a platform for growth, visibility, and impact. By applying, you gain the opportunity to: Advance your career in archival studies Bring your archival project to life Connect with leading professionals in the field Contribute to preserving and amplifying underrepresented histories For aspiring archivists committed to community memory and social justice, this scholarship represents a meaningful step forward. For more information click here Discover more opportunities here The post NAP Scholarship 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for Archivists of African Descent to Advance Their Careers and Projects appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: CARIBEquity Investment Training Program 2026: Strengthening Investor Capacity and Unlocking Capital in the Caribbean
summary: The Caribbean region is rich in untapped capital and emerging innovation. However, despite this potential, one of the key challenges facing the region is not the absence of financial resources, but rather the lack of shared investment frameworks, investor confidence, and practical tools to effectively deploy capital. The CARIBEquity Investment Training Program 2026 has been developed to directly address these challenges. This initiative is a strategic effort aimed at strengthening investor capacity, improving coordination among regional stakeholders, and facilitating smarter, more impactful capital allocation into early-stage ventures. Applications are currently open and will remain available until April 10, 2026 . Understanding the Need: Why This Program Matters While capital exists across the Caribbean, investment ecosystems often remain fragmented. This fragmentation leads to: Limited collaboration between investors Inconsistent investment practices Reduced trust in early-stage ventures Missed opportunities for innovation-driven growth The CARIBEquity initiative recognizes that building a strong investment ecosystem requires more than funding. It requires education, alignment, and confidence among investors. What is the CARIBEquity Investment Training Program? The CARIBEquity Investment Training Program is a specialized capacity-building initiative designed to equip investors and ecosystem stakeholders with the knowledge and tools necessary to engage effectively in early-stage investing. Developed alongside the CARIBEquity Investor Toolkit , the program forms part of a broader, long-term strategy to strengthen the Caribbean’s entrepreneurial and investment landscape. Core Objectives of the Program The program is structured around three key goals: 1. Building Trust in Early-Stage Investing By providing practical knowledge and proven frameworks, the program helps investors better understand and manage the risks associated with early-stage ventures. 2. Reducing Market Fragmentation The initiative promotes alignment among investors, institutions, and ecosystem actors, fostering a more cohesive regional investment environment. 3. Mobilizing Capital Locally and Regionally By enhancing investor confidence and capability, the program encourages more capital to be deployed within the Caribbean, supporting innovation and economic growth. Who Should Apply? The program is specifically designed for individuals and organizations involved in or interested in investment activities within the Caribbean ecosystem. Ideal participants include: Investors seeking to expand into early-stage opportunities High-Net-Worth Individuals (HNWIs) exploring venture investments Corporate leaders interested in innovation and strategic investments Financial institutions aiming to support startup ecosystems Ecosystem partners working to strengthen regional entrepreneurship What Participants Will Gain Participants in the CARIBEquity Investment Training Program will benefit from: A deeper understanding of early-stage investment strategies Access to practical tools and frameworks for decision-making Insights into regional market dynamics Opportunities to connect with key stakeholders and investors Enhanced confidence in deploying capital effectively Program Partners and Implementation The CARIBEquity initiative is part of a collaborative effort involving international and regional actors committed to strengthening economic development in the Caribbean. The program is: Co-funded by the European Union and IDB Lab Implemented by Seedstars and EOG (Entrepreneurs Of Greatness) This collaboration ensures a high-quality, globally informed, and locally relevant training experience. Application Deadline Deadline to Apply: April 10, 2026 Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications early to secure their place in this competitive program. Why You Should Apply The CARIBEquity Investment Training Program represents a unique opportunity to: Strengthen your investment knowledge and skills Contribute to the growth of the Caribbean innovation ecosystem Build valuable connections with regional and international stakeholders Play a role in shaping the future of investment in emerging markets For anyone serious about investing in innovation and driving economic transformation in the Caribbean, this program offers both strategic insight and practical value. APPLY HERE Discover more opportunities here The post CARIBEquity Investment Training Program 2026: Strengthening Investor Capacity and Unlocking Capital in the Caribbean appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Black in Business Program 2026: A Fully Funded Opportunity to Grow Your Business, Increase Revenue, and Create Jobs
summary: In today’s competitive entrepreneurial landscape, many small business owners struggle not because of lack of passion, but because of limited access to structured business education, financial literacy, and growth strategies. The Black in Business Program 2026 is designed to bridge that gap. This free business education program is specifically tailored to empower sole proprietors with the practical tools, knowledge, and confidence needed to transform their businesses into sustainable and scalable ventures. Applications are now officially open for the Fall 2026 cohort , offering entrepreneurs a unique opportunity to sharpen their business skills and accelerate growth without any financial burden. What is the Black in Business Program? The Black in Business Program is a comprehensive entrepreneurship initiative focused on helping sole proprietors build stronger, more resilient businesses. It goes beyond theory by providing actionable insights that participants can immediately apply to their businesses. The program is designed to support entrepreneurs in: Scaling their operations Increasing profitability Creating employment opportunities Strengthening long-term business sustainability Through structured learning and practical application, participants gain a deeper understanding of how to run a successful and growth-oriented business. Why This Program Matters Entrepreneurship plays a crucial role in economic development, innovation, and job creation. However, many entrepreneurs face common challenges such as: Difficulty pricing products or services correctly Lack of financial management skills Limited knowledge of hiring and team building Uncertainty about scaling strategies The Black in Business Program directly addresses these challenges by equipping participants with essential business tools and frameworks. Program Impact: Proven Results The effectiveness of the program is reflected in the success of its alumni. According to recent data: 65% of participants reported increased revenues 90% believe the program enhanced their ability to innovate 94% feel optimistic about their future business growth These results highlight the program’s ability to create tangible and lasting impact on entrepreneurs and their businesses. What You Will Learn The curriculum is carefully designed to cover the most critical aspects of running and growing a business. Participants will gain practical knowledge in areas such as: 1. Pricing Strategy Learn how to price your products or services competitively while ensuring profitability. 2. Financial Management Understand your business finances, manage cash flow, and make data-driven decisions. 3. Hiring and Team Building Gain insights into when and how to hire your first employee and build a strong team. 4. Business Growth Strategies Explore proven techniques to scale your business and expand your market reach. 5. Innovation and Sustainability Develop the ability to innovate and adapt in a rapidly changing business environment. Program Structure and Commitment Participants are expected to actively engage throughout the program. This includes: Attending learning sessions Completing assignments or practical exercises Applying concepts directly to their businesses The structured format ensures that participants not only learn but also implement strategies in real time. Program Timeline Applications Open: Now Cohort: Fall 2026 (Applicants are encouraged to apply early as spaces may be limited.) How to Apply Interested applicants can submit their application through the official platform, Make sure to prepare relevant information about your business, including your goals and current challenges, as this will strengthen your application. Why You Should Apply The Black in Business Program stands out because it is: Completely free of charge Focused on practical, real-world business skills Designed to deliver measurable business growth Backed by proven success metrics If you are a sole proprietor looking to take your business to the next level, this program offers a powerful opportunity to gain the tools and confidence needed to succeed. title: Powerful Georgetown Law Investigative Internship Program 2026 for Future Legal Professionals
summary: The Powerful Georgetown Law Investigative Internship Program 2026 offers an exceptional hands-on opportunity for students and recent graduates interested in criminal law, public policy, and public interest work. Hosted by Georgetown University’s Criminal Justice Clinic, this internship provides real-world investigative experience supporting active criminal defense cases in Washington, DC. With a strong legacy dating back to 1985, the Investigative Internship Program plays a critical role in ensuring high-quality legal representation for indigent defendants. Interns work closely with attorneys across multiple clinics, contributing directly to case preparation, client advocacy, and justice outcomes. Applications for the Fall 2026 intake are currently open, with a final deadline of May 15, 2026. However, due to rolling admissions, applicants are strongly encouraged to apply early. About the Georgetown Law Investigative Internship Program The Georgetown Law Investigative Internship Program was established to support the work of the Criminal Justice Clinic, which has been providing legal representation to underserved communities since 1971. Today, the program supports three key clinics: • Criminal Defense Prisoner Advocacy Clinic • Criminal Justice Clinic • Juvenile Justice Clinic Interns are integrated into legal teams and play an active role in investigative work that directly impacts the outcome of criminal cases. The program emphasizes experiential learning, allowing participants to engage deeply with the legal system and understand the realities of criminal defense work. Hands-On Legal and Investigative Experience This internship is highly practical and immersive, giving participants the opportunity to work on real cases from start to finish. Interns conduct field investigations in pairs and contribute to every stage of case preparation. Key responsibilities include: • Locating and interviewing witnesses in communities and correctional facilities • Drafting statements and legal memoranda for court use • Conducting criminal background checks • Reviewing and analyzing police and surveillance footage • Collecting and summarizing case-related documents • Documenting and analyzing crime scenes • Creating evidence materials for trial Interns are also involved in high-level legal processes such as client consultations, plea negotiations, and strategy sessions with attorneys. Holistic Approach to Criminal Defense A unique feature of the Georgetown Investigative Internship Program is its holistic approach to criminal defense. The program recognizes that effective advocacy extends beyond legal representation and addresses broader social challenges faced by clients. Interns assist clients with: • Accessing housing support • Enrolling in treatment and rehabilitation programs • Preparing for reintegration into society This approach ensures that clients are supported as individuals, not just as legal cases, and provides interns with a deeper understanding of the social dimensions of justice. Trial Preparation and Courtroom Exposure Interns play a crucial role in trial preparation, assisting attorneys with last-minute case developments and logistical coordination. They help ensure that defense witnesses are prepared, available, and able to attend court proceedings. In some cases, interns may even be called to testify, providing them with rare and valuable courtroom experience. Additionally, interns contribute to post-conviction cases, including compassionate release applications and resentencing efforts under key legal frameworks such as IRAA and SLAA. Learning and Professional Development The internship offers a highly educational environment where participants work alongside experienced attorneys, law students, and faculty members. Interns gain: • A deep understanding of the criminal legal system • Practical investigative and legal skills • Exposure to real-world legal challenges • Insight into careers in law and public service The program also provides mentorship and career guidance, helping interns make informed decisions about pursuing legal careers. Who Should Apply The Powerful Georgetown Law Investigative Internship Program 2026 is ideal for individuals considering careers in law, criminal justice, investigative journalism, or public policy. Applicants may include: • Undergraduate students (especially juniors and seniors) • Recent graduates • Graduate students No specific major is required, and applicants from diverse academic backgrounds are encouraged to apply. While prior experience in law or criminal justice is helpful, it is not mandatory. Applicants should demonstrate strong communication skills, the ability to work in diverse environments, and a willingness to engage in field-based investigative work. Important Considerations This internship is intensive and may involve long or irregular working hours, particularly during trial preparation periods. It also requires fieldwork in urban communities, which may be unfamiliar to some applicants. However, interns receive training and work in pairs to ensure safety and support throughout their experience. Application Process Applicants must complete an online application form and may submit additional materials such as writing samples, publications, or project work to strengthen their application. The selection process evaluates candidates based on: • Quality of application materials • Academic and professional background • Communication skills • References and supporting documents Shortlisted candidates may be invited for video interviews. Deadline for Applications The final deadline to apply for the Powerful Georgetown Law Investigative Internship Program 2026 is May 15, 2026. Due to the rolling selection process, early applications are strongly recommended. Learn More About This Opportunity To learn more about the Powerful Georgetown Law Investigative Internship Program 2026 and apply, click here Conclusion The Georgetown Law Investigative Internship Program 2026 is a powerful opportunity for aspiring legal professionals to gain real-world experience in criminal defense and investigative work. By participating in this program, interns develop critical skills, contribute to meaningful legal cases, and gain a deeper understanding of justice and advocacy. For more internship opportunities on OFY Opportunities click here The post Powerful Georgetown Law Investigative Internship Program 2026 for Future Legal Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: EU-Africa Cooperation in Horizon Europe Work Programme 2026-2027 (Research and Innovation : Africa Initiative IV)
summary: If you are searching for a life-changing international opportunity backed by one of the world’s most influential institutions, this detailed guide explores a major initiative published by the European Union through the Publications Office of the European Union . This program focuses on vocational education, skills development, and capacity building across partner countries, making it highly relevant for students, professionals, policymakers, and institutions worldwide. What Is This European Union Opportunity About? This opportunity is rooted in a strategic EU initiative aimed at strengthening vocational education and training (VET) systems globally, especially in developing and partner countries. According to the official EU publication, the program provides conceptual frameworks, operational guidance, and practical tools for implementing skills development projects. The initiative is designed to support: Skills development for employment Workforce readiness Economic growth through technical education Inclusive access to training opportunities The EU uses this framework to guide its development cooperation projects, ensuring that education and training systems align with labor market demands. Why This Opportunity Matters Globally The European Union has positioned vocational education as a cornerstone for sustainable development. This program is not just theoretical—it actively informs real funding opportunities, grants, and projects implemented worldwide. Key objectives include: 1. Promoting Inclusive Growth The program focuses on ensuring that marginalized groups, including youth and disadvantaged populations, gain access to relevant skills training. 2. Strengthening Education Systems It helps governments and institutions design, implement, and evaluate effective vocational training systems. 3. Supporting Employment and Entrepreneurship By aligning training with industry needs, the initiative improves employability and encourages entrepreneurship. 4. Enhancing International Cooperation The EU collaborates with partner countries to co-develop sustainable education and training policies. Who Can Apply or Benefit? This opportunity is not limited to individuals—it is designed for a wide ecosystem of stakeholders. Eligible Categories of Applicants Students and trainees in vocational fields Educational institutions (TVET colleges, universities) Government agencies NGOs and development organizations Training providers Policy makers and education planners Eligible Countries and Regions This EU initiative is global in scope, but primarily targets: Africa Asia Latin America EU partner countries It is especially relevant for developing countries and regions receiving EU development assistance. Key Qualifications and Requirements While specific requirements vary depending on the individual project or grant call, the general expectations include: For Individuals: Interest or background in vocational education or technical skills Enrollment in or completion of relevant training programs Willingness to participate in development-focused initiatives For Institutions and Organizations: Experience in education, training, or workforce development Capacity to implement training programs Alignment with EU development priorities Ability to monitor and evaluate project outcomes What Does the Program Offer? Depending on the specific EU-funded project, participants or institutions may receive: 1. Financial Support Grants for training programs Funding for institutional development Scholarships (in some cases tied to projects) 2. Technical Assistance Access to EU expertise Training materials and frameworks Policy guidance and implementation tools 3. Capacity Building Institutional strengthening Curriculum development Monitoring and evaluation systems 4. Networking Opportunities Collaboration with international stakeholders Partnerships with EU institutions Access to global development networks How the Program Works The EU does not always provide direct applications through a single portal for this initiative. Instead, it operates through: EU-funded projects in partner countries Calls for proposals issued by EU delegations Partnerships with local governments and organizations The publication emphasizes that the framework supports all stages of project implementation, including: Identification of needs Program design Execution Monitoring and evaluation Key Benefits of Participating Access to internationally recognized training systems Increased employability and technical skills Exposure to global best practices Opportunities for funding and institutional growth Contribution to national and regional development Step-by-Step Guide to Access This Opportunity Step 1: Monitor EU Funding Calls Check EU funding platforms and local EU delegation announcements. Step 2: Identify Relevant Project @ Look for vocational education, skills training, or development-related programs. Step 3: Apply Through Implementing Partners Most opportunities are administered by local institutions or NGOs funded by the EU. Step 4: Prepare Required Documents Academic or professional credentials Project proposals (for organizations) Proof of eligibility Apply here Important Deadline Information Deadline: Varies by project Many EU-funded programs operate on rolling or periodic calls It is critical to regularly check updates from EU delegations in your country or region. The European Union’s vocational education and training initiative is more than just a program—it is a global development strategy that opens doors for individuals, institutions, and governments. For more information visit here For more global opportunities click here The post EU-Africa Cooperation in Horizon Europe Work Programme 2026-2027 (Research and Innovation : Africa Initiative IV) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Apply Now: Africa AI Policy Fellowship 2026 for Legal, Policy and Tech Professionals
summary: As Artificial Intelligence continues to reshape industries worldwide, Africa faces a growing need for skilled professionals who can guide its development responsibly. In response, Lawyers Hub has launched the Africa AI Policy Fellowship 2026 —a flagship initiative aimed at nurturing the continent’s next generation of AI governance and regulatory leaders. This prestigious three-month fellowship is designed to bridge the gap between emerging technologies and effective policymaking, ensuring that AI systems across Africa are ethical, inclusive, and contextually relevant. Why the Africa AI Policy Fellowship Matters The rapid evolution of AI technologies has outpaced many existing regulatory frameworks. This fellowship equips participants with the expertise needed to influence policy decisions and build resilient governance systems tailored to African realities. Through the program, fellows will gain hands-on experience in: AI Governance Frameworks – Understanding the legal and regulatory landscape of AI Ethical Impact Analysis – Evaluating the societal and economic implications of AI systems Contextual Policy Design – Developing solutions aligned with Africa’s unique challenges Professional Skill Development – Enhancing research, analytical thinking, and technical writing Who Should Apply? The fellowship targets emerging and mid-career professionals who are passionate about shaping the future of technology policy in Africa. Ideal applicants include: Policy analysts and advisors interested in tech governance Legal professionals and regulatory experts Researchers and academics focused on digital transformation Innovation specialists and development practitioners Key Benefits of the Fellowship Participants will gain access to a robust ecosystem designed to accelerate their careers and impact: Expert Mentorship – Learn from leading African and global AI policy experts Practical Experience – Contribute to real-world policy initiatives Pan-African Networking – Connect with professionals across tech and policy sectors Career Growth Opportunities – Strengthen your profile in governance and emerging technologies How to Apply The application process is straightforward and designed to identify high-potential candidates: Complete the Online Application – A short form that takes approximately 7 minutes Submit a Statement of Interest – Clearly outline your passion for AI policy and career goals Highlight Relevant Experience – Showcase your professional achievements and background APPLY HERE Take the Next Step If you’re ready to play a role in shaping Africa’s digital future, the Africa AI Policy Fellowship 2026 offers a unique platform to build your expertise and influence meaningful change. Visit the official application portal to get started and take the first step toward becoming a leader in AI policy and governance in Africa. title: World Food Programme Internship 2026: Digital Media and Communications Role in Ethiopia
summary: The World Food Programme (WFP) is inviting applications for its Digital Communications Internship based in Addis Ababa, Ethiopia. This opportunity is ideal for students and recent graduates in communications, journalism, and related fields who are passionate about humanitarian work and storytelling. WFP is committed to diversity, inclusion, and equal opportunity, encouraging applications from qualified candidates regardless of background. About the World Food Programme (WFP) The World Food Programme (WFP) is the world’s largest humanitarian organization, dedicated to saving lives in emergencies and using food assistance to support long-term recovery, stability, and prosperity. As a 2020 Nobel Peace Prize Laureate , WFP operates globally to assist communities affected by conflict, disasters, and climate change. The organization prioritizes a people-centered approach, building diverse and high-performing teams guided by its core values: Integrity Collaboration Commitment Humanity Inclusion Why Join WFP? Joining WFP offers more than just work experience—it’s a chance to contribute to meaningful global impact. Key benefits include: A diverse and inclusive multicultural work environment Access to training, mentorship, and professional development programs Opportunities for career growth and internal mobility Collaboration with passionate professionals across global offices Competitive compensation package Internship Overview Position: Intern (Digital Communications) Location: Addis Ababa, Ethiopia Duration: 8 Months Eligibility: Open to Ethiopian nationals Deadline: 30th March 2026 Role Purpose Under the supervision of the communications team, the intern will support the creation of innovative, high-quality multimedia content targeted at key audiences such as donors, partners, government stakeholders, and beneficiaries. Key Responsibilities As a Digital Communications Intern, you will: Develop communication materials including social media content, stories, and visuals Collect field content such as photos, testimonials, and quotes Draft content for digital platforms under supervision Ensure consistency in branding and messaging Coordinate with designers, vendors, and printers Support social media scheduling, monitoring, and reporting Provide general administrative support for digital communications Perform additional communication-related tasks as assigned Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet one of the following conditions : Currently enrolled in an undergraduate program (with at least 2 years completed) Currently enrolled in a graduate program Recent graduate (within 6 months) in communications, journalism, or a related field Additional requirements: Fluency in English and Amharic (written and spoken) Ability to work in Addis Ababa Must not be an immediate family member of a WFP employee Required Skills and Competencies Ideal candidates should have: Proficiency in tools such as Adobe Premiere Pro, InDesign, Illustrator, Photoshop, After Effects, Lightroom, Canva , and MS Office Strong digital storytelling and content creation skills Understanding of communication strategies and humanitarian contexts A creative portfolio demonstrating attention to detail Ability to work independently and in diverse teams Flexibility and adaptability in a dynamic work environment WFP Leadership Framework WFP operates under a leadership framework that defines expected behaviors and standards for collaboration and impact. Candidates are encouraged to familiarize themselves with these principles before applying. Diversity, Inclusion Equal Opportunity WFP is an equal opportunity employer committed to creating an inclusive workplace. Applications are encouraged from individuals of all backgrounds, including persons with disabilities. Reasonable accommodations are available throughout the recruitment process. Important Application Information Before applying, take note of the following: Applications must be submitted through WFP’s official online recruitment system Required documents: CV and Cover Letter Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted WFP does not charge any fees at any stage of the recruitment process Application Tips Ensure your profile is complete and up to date (education, experience, skills) Tailor your CV and cover letter to highlight digital communication skills and creativity Include a portfolio showcasing your work if available APPLY HERE Start Your Humanitarian Career with WFP This internship is a unique opportunity to gain hands-on experience in digital communications while contributing to life-saving humanitarian efforts. If you are passionate about storytelling, media, and global impact, this could be your pathway into the international development sector. Discover more similar opportunities here. title: Apply Now: ACNEI 2026 Free Online Course in Computational Neuroscience for African Students
summary: The African Computational Neuroscience Educational Initiative (ACNEI) is inviting applications for its 10-Day Introductory Online Training Program under the theme: “Foundations of Computational Neuroscience for Young African Scholars.” This unique program is designed to inspire and equip the next generation of African thinkers with foundational skills in neuroscience, computation, and data-driven problem solving. Computational neuroscience is a rapidly growing interdisciplinary field that blends brain science, artificial intelligence, data science, and medicine . However, access to structured learning opportunities in this field remains limited across much of Africa. ACNEI aims to bridge this gap by democratizing access to high-quality training and preparing students for future careers in neuroscience and AI-related disciplines. Program Overview Program Dates: 11th – 22nd May 2026 Optional Regional Hub Training: 25th – 28th May 2026 Format: Fully Online Duration: 2 weeks (2 hours per day) Target Audience: Post-high school students and early undergraduate students across Africa Cost: Free (selection-based) What You Will Learn Participants in the ACNEI Introductory School will gain: A foundational understanding of how the brain works and why computation is essential Beginner-friendly Python programming skills tailored for neuroscience applications Insights into neurons and neural networks as computational systems Hands-on experience through mini-projects (no prior coding experience required) Exposure to African-centered neuroscience and AI case studies Mentorship and career guidance from global experts in the field Why You Should Apply This program is more than just an introduction—it is a gateway into one of the most exciting and impactful fields shaping the future of science and technology. Whether you aspire to work in AI, healthcare, research, or data science , this training provides the foundational knowledge, mentorship, and network needed to get started. If you are a young African student curious about how the brain works and how technology can help us understand it, this is your opportunity to begin that journey. Who Should Apply? ACNEI strongly encourages applications from: African post-high school students, gap-year students, and early undergraduates Students interested in biology, medicine, engineering, computer science, psychology, artificial intelligence, neuroscience, or data science Applicants with little or no prior coding experience Individuals from underrepresented regions and backgrounds across Africa Benefits of the Program Participants selected for the program will receive: A Certificate of Completion Access to the ACNEI mentorship and alumni network Priority consideration for advanced ACNEI programs and regional training hubs Exposure to global open-science and neuroscience communities Application Details Application Deadline: March 29th, 2026 Program Start Date: May 11th, 2026 Interested applicants are encouraged to apply before the deadline to secure their spot in this competitive and impactful program. Apply Here Discover similar opportunities here. The post Apply Now: ACNEI 2026 Free Online Course in Computational Neuroscience for African Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Youth Policy Development Group 2026: Empower the Next Generation of UK Policy Leaders
summary: Are you a young person in the UK ready to make your voice heard where it matters most? The Youth Policy Development Group (YPDG) 2026 , relaunched by Shout Out UK, is your gateway to influencing national conversations, shaping policy, and building real-world skills in governance and advocacy. This is more than just a program—it’s a platform for young leaders to contribute directly to decision-making processes and represent the views of their generation. What is the Youth Policy Development Group (YPDG)? The Youth Policy Development Group (YPDG) is a dynamic initiative designed to bring together passionate individuals aged 14–25 from across the UK. Participants collaborate on policy research, engage in meaningful discussions, and present their findings directly to policymakers and government officials. Through this program, you will: Conduct in-depth policy research on key national issues Collaborate with a diverse group of young thinkers Present policy recommendations to decision-makers Participate in roundtable discussions and live events Why You Should Apply Joining YPDG offers a range of valuable benefits that can shape your future: 1. Direct Impact on Policy Have your ideas heard by politicians and government departments, ensuring youth perspectives are included in national decisions. 2. Skill Development Enhance your knowledge in political literacy, media awareness, research, and public speaking. 3. Professional Training Mentorship Receive structured online training sessions and one-on-one support from experienced professionals at Shout Out UK. 4. Networking Opportunities Connect with policymakers, activists, and like-minded young leaders across the UK. 5. Personal Growth Build confidence, leadership skills, and a strong understanding of civic engagement. What to Expect from the Programme As a YPDG participant, you will: Work on policy briefs addressing real-world issues Attend training sessions to strengthen your analytical and communication skills Receive personalized feedback and mentorship Engage in in-person and virtual discussions Represent youth voices in high-level conversations Eligibility Criteria To apply, you must: Be aged 14–25 years Currently reside in the United Kingdom (England, Scotland, Wales, or Northern Ireland) Have a passion for policy, research, and social impact Application Deadline Make sure to submit your application before the deadline: Deadline: Sunday, 29th March 2026 Late applications will not be considered, so apply early to secure your spot. How to Apply Interested candidates can express their interest by completing the official application form: APPLY HERE. For inquiries: contact@shoutoutuk.org For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more fellowship opportunities, click here . Final Thoughts If you’re passionate about shaping the future and want to contribute meaningfully to national discussions, the Youth Policy Development Group 2026 is the perfect opportunity. Step forward, share your ideas, and become part of a movement that empowers young voices across the UK. title: Visiting Researchers Program 2026/2027 at the Max Planck Institute for the Study of Societies (MPIfG)
summary: The Max Planck Institute for the Study of Societies (MPIfG) is now accepting applications for its prestigious Visiting Researchers Program for the winter semester 2026/2027. This is an excellent opportunity for scholars in economic sociology and political economy to engage in high-level research within one of the world’s leading institutes for social science research. 🧠 About the Program The MPIfG conducts cutting-edge research on the governance of modern societies, focusing on the intersection of social, economic, and political systems. The Visiting Researchers Program is designed to foster collaboration and intellectual exchange among global scholars working in: Economic Sociology Comparative Political Economy International Political Economy Participants will be integrated into a dynamic research environment and collaborate with leading experts in the field. ⏳ Duration of Stay Selected visiting researchers can stay at MPIfG for a period ranging from 2 to 12 months during the winter semester 2026/2027. 👩‍🏫 Who Can Apply? Applications are welcome from: Professors Postdoctoral researchers Doctoral researchers Candidates from all countries are encouraged to apply, provided their research aligns with MPIfG’s core themes and ongoing projects. 📄 Application Requirements Applicants must submit: A research proposal (maximum 3 pages) outlining their intended project A funding plan (as candidates are required to secure their own funding) An indication of a preferred MPIfG director or research group leader Successful applicants will receive an official invitation letter to support their external funding applications. 🏆 Selection Criteria Candidates will be selected based on: Academic excellence Relevance and quality of the research proposal Alignment with MPIfG’s research agenda 🌐 Research Environment Benefits Access to a collaborative, interdisciplinary academic community Engagement with leading scholars in sociology and political science Opportunities to contribute to ongoing research discussions and projects Supportive and inclusive institutional culture promoting equality and diversity ⚠️ Important Notes Visiting researchers must arrange their own funding Accepted stay periods cannot be postponed or split Applications must be submitted online only (email submissions are not accepted) 📅 Application Deadline The deadline to apply is April 30, 2026 (23:59 UTC+2) . Late submissions will not be considered. 🔗 How to Apply Interested candidates should visit the official MPIfG website to access the application portal and detailed guidelines. APPLY HERE.
